display.purpose: Page titles only — one per view, via PageHeader.

body.purpose: Default UI text, table cells, labels — the workhorse face.

mono.purpose: Scannable technical values: IPs, UUIDs, sizes, counts. Tabular-nums always on.

shadows.name: raised
shadows.purpose: Resting state for cards, tables, stat tiles — barely visible separation cue.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Drawers, dialogs, dropdowns — anything floating above the page.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Default easing for row-action reveal, bento-tile hover lift, bulk-bar slide-up.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one primary action per view (Save, Create).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: #4f46e5; color: #ffffff; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; border: none; transition: background 150ms; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #4338ca; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #4f46e5; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { cursor: not-allowed; opacity: 0.6; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: #ffffff; color: #4a4f63; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #e3e6ef; transition: background 150ms; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #eef0f7; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #4f46e5; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Semantic status/criticality/environment indicator with a matching dot.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; border-radius: 6px; padding: 4px 8px; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.01em; line-height: 1; background: #d1fae5; color: #059669; } .ds-badge-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #059669; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Default container for grouped content — flat at rest.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid rgba(227,230,239,0.7); border-radius: 12px; padding: 20px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(15,18,34,.04), 0 4px 12px -4px rgba(15,18,34,.08); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Default form field with soft accent glow on focus.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #ffffff; color: #0f1222; border: 1px solid #e3e6ef; border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px 12px; font-size: 14px; transition: border-color 150ms, box-shadow 150ms; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #9aa0b4; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #4f46e5; box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(79,70,229,0.12); }

components.name: Table Row
components.description: Data row with hidden-until-hover actions and optional status-color left accent.
components.css: .ds-row { transition: background 200ms; } .ds-row:nth-child(odd) { background: #ffffff; } .ds-row:nth-child(even) { background: rgba(246,247,251,0.6); } .ds-row:hover { background: rgba(79,70,229,0.05); } .ds-cell { padding: 12px 16px; font-size: 14px; color: #0f1222; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-row-actions { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(2px); transition: opacity 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-row:hover .ds-row-actions { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); }

narrative.overview: InventoryMGR is read by sysadmins mid-task, not browsed by visitors being sold something. The system takes its cue from an instrument panel: calm, neutral chrome everywhere, with color used only where it encodes a real reading — a VM's status, a host's criticality, an environment tier. Nothing on screen competes with that signal. The palette outside the semantic layer is deliberately quiet (near-white surfaces, slate-toned text, a single indigo accent for interactive elements), so that when a badge turns red or amber, it reads immediately as meaning something, not as decoration. This explicitly rejects the bloated-enterprise-SaaS look — dense chrome, modal-on-modal flows, buried actions, decorative gradients. A two-second edit should never feel like a form wizard. Depth and motion are used the same way color is: sparingly, and only in response to state (hover, focus, a row revealing its actions), never as base decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Neutral chrome, saturated signal — color is reserved for data meaning (status/criticality/environment/platform), never decoration
Flat by default; elevation appears only on interaction (hover lift, focus ring, drawer/overlay)
Dense information display (tables, badges, mono technical values) with enough breathing room to avoid feeling cramped
Full light/dark parity — every semantic color has a tuned dark-mode counterpart, not just an inverted background

rules.name: The Signal Rule
rules.body: Saturated color appears only where it encodes a real, current data value — a VM's status, a host's criticality tier, an environment or platform badge. It never appears as page chrome, decoration, or emphasis-for-emphasis's-sake. If a color can't be traced to a specific field's value, it doesn't belong on screen.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Any value a user might scan down a column (IP address, size, count, UUID) gets tabular-nums and the mono stack, so digits align vertically. Never render technical values in the body font.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Nothing gets a shadow just for existing. A card's shadow announces 'this is a distinct region,' not 'this is important.' Elevation increases only with genuine z-axis meaning: hover, overlay, never as a styling flourish on a static element.

dos: Do reserve saturated color for the six semantic categories (status, criticality, environment, platform, os_family, lifecycle) — every other surface stays neutral.
Do keep shadows nearly invisible at rest and reserve shadow-overlay for genuinely floating surfaces.
Do use the mono/tabular-nums stack for any scannable technical value.
Do keep the primary button to one per view.
Do hide row actions until hover/focus so tables stay dense and quiet until needed.
donts: Don't build a bloated-enterprise-SaaS interface — no dense chrome, no modal-on-modal flows, no burying the primary action behind menus.
Don't add shadows to a static element 'for depth.'
Don't add a second left-border color accent pattern outside the existing status-row convention.
Don't turn a routine two-second edit into a multi-step wizard or add a confirmation modal where none is currently required.
